steps.title: Identify the present-time marker
steps.content: The word Today at the start of the second sentence marks the present time. The sentence structure is subject + blank (form of be) + past participle (considered) + object—a passive voice construction.

steps.title: Match the tense to the time marker
steps.content: The rule is that verb tense must match the time reference in the sentence. Since Today marks the present time, we need the present tense form of be, which is is. The other options violate this rule by using different tenses: will be (future), had been (past perfect), and was (simple past). That matches choice D — D is correct.
